name: Dr. Sylvia Blyden
bio: Dr. Sylvia Blyden is a renowned Sierra Leonean public health physician, journalist, and politician with over two decades of service in health advocacy and public policy. A graduate of Fourah Bay College and London, she has championed healthcare reform across Sierra Leone.

name: Dr. Alpha Tejan-Jalloh
bio: Dr. Alpha Tejan-Jalloh is one of Sierra Leone's foremost cardiologists, trained at the University of Sierra Leone and specialized in the UK. He leads the cardiac unit at Connaught Hospital and has pioneered heart disease prevention campaigns.
qualifications: Fellowship in Cardiology - Royal College of Physicians

name: Dr. Amara Jambai
hospital: National Public Health Agency of Sierra Leone
bio: Dr. Amara Jambai is Sierra Leone's leading epidemiologist and former Director of Preventive Health Services. He was instrumental in Sierra Leone's Ebola response and continues to lead national infectious disease surveillance programmes.

name: Dr. Yvonne Carew
bio: Dr. Yvonne Carew is a leading OB-GYN at PCMH, specializing in high-risk pregnancies, reproductive health, and women's health advocacy. She has reduced maternal mortality rates in her department by over 30% through protocol reforms.

name: Dr. Bankole Kargbo
bio: Dr. Bankole Kargbo is a dedicated pediatrician at Ola During Children's Hospital, Sierra Leone's premier children's health facility. He specializes in childhood malnutrition, neonatal care, and pediatric infectious diseases.
qualifications: Fellowship in Pediatrics - West African College of Physicians

name: Dr. Fatmata Koroma-Bangura
bio: Dr. Fatmata Koroma-Bangura specializes in comprehensive reproductive healthcare including family planning, post-abortion care, safe abortion services under Sierra Leone's Safe Abortion Act 2023, and maternal health. She is committed to reducing maternal deaths from unsafe procedures.
qualifications: Training in Safe Abortion Care - WHO Protocol

name: Dr. John Sisay
bio: Dr. John Sisay is a distinguished orthopedic and trauma surgeon with two decades of surgical excellence in Sierra Leone. He trained in Nigeria and the UK and serves as Chief Surgeon at Choithram Hospital.

name: Dr. Abu Bakarr Fofanah
bio: Dr. Abu Bakarr Fofanah is one of a small number of trained neurologists in Sierra Leone. He specializes in stroke management, epilepsy, and neurodegenerative diseases, and runs the only dedicated neurology clinic in Freetown.
qualifications: Fellowship in Neurology - Royal College of Physicians

name: Dr. Memunatu Pratt
bio: Dr. Memunatu Pratt is a leading dermatologist specializing in tropical skin conditions, albinism care, wound management, and cosmetic dermatology. She leads the Skin Health Initiative at Lumley Hospital.

name: Dr. Ibrahim Koroma
bio: Dr. Ibrahim Koroma is Sierra Leone's leading ophthalmologist, specializing in cataract surgery, glaucoma management, and preventable blindness. He has performed over 3,000 sight-restoring surgeries.

name: Dr. Agnes Macauley
bio: Dr. Agnes Macauley is a consultant psychiatrist and Sierra Leone's foremost mental health advocate. She has led mental health reforms after the Ebola crisis and specializes in post-traumatic stress, depression, and community mental health.

name: Dr. Edward Nahim
bio: Dr. Edward Nahim is one of Sierra Leone's most senior surgeons with over 24 years of clinical experience. He specialises in emergency surgery, laparoscopy, and abdominal procedures, and has trained over 30 junior surgeons.
